info.label: What
info.value: The same menu an AI agent or script drives non-interactively: JSON in, JSON out. No TUI needed.

info.label: 1. Discover
info.value: menuki list --config <this>   lists every action with its id, description and typed-input contract.

info.label: 3. Parse
info.value: Actions can emit structured JSON (format/query) so the agent reads a result, not a screen.

items.name: Get status
items.description: Return service status as a JSON object. The agent parses stdout directly.
items.help: Uses format:json so the shell output is pretty-printed and machine-readable. Try: menuki exec --config <this> --action main/get-status

items.name: List services
items.description: Return just the service names, extracted from JSON with a JSONPath query.
items.help: query pulls $.services[*].name out of the command's JSON before it is returned - the agent gets an array, not the whole payload.

items.name: Recent events
items.description: Return a JSON array of flat rows rendered as a table.
items.help: format:table turns a JSON array of objects into aligned columns - handy for a human reading the agent's transcript.

items.name: Restart service
items.description: Restart a named service. The name is validated before the command runs.
items.help: A typed input gives the agent a precise contract from `list`: pass --param service=<name>; only lowercase names with hyphens are accepted.

items.name: Scale service
items.description: Set replica count for a service, both values validated.
items.help: Two typed inputs: a choice (fixed set) and a bounded number. The agent learns the allowed options and range from `list` and cannot pass anything invalid.
